title: OpenAI Stops Sora App and Disney Deal
summary: OpenAI has closed its Sora app, which made videos using AI from simple words. It also ended a partnership with Disney, a big entertainment company. OpenAI says it wants to focus on other projects like robots that can help people with real tasks. Disney respects this decision and will work with other AI companies to use technology safely without breaking rules. Sora started in 2024 and made very realistic videos, but some people worried about copyright issues. Disney had a deal with OpenAI to let users make videos with characters like Mickey Mouse. Now, OpenAI will not make video tools anymore but will keep working on other AI technologies.

an app that created videos using AI

artificial intelligence, which helps computers think and create

questions.question: Which company ended its partnership with OpenAI?
What characters were in the Disney deal?

background_read: OpenAI is a tech company known for AI tools like ChatGPT. Sora was an app that let people type words to make AI videos. Disney is a famous entertainment company with characters like Mickey Mouse. A partnership means companies agree to work together. Copyright is a law that protects creative work from being copied without permission. Robots are machines that can do tasks automatically.
Article_Structure: WHAT: OpenAI closed Sora app and ended partnership with Disney
WHERE: Not specified in the summary
WHY: To focus on other AI projects like robots

perspectives.description: Wants to shift to new technologies like robots to help people.
Respects the decision and will find other AI partners to use technology responsibly.
